test.name: Historical person lookup
test.description: Wikipedia lookup for a person. Tests the same workflow as place lookups but on a person-shaped article. Slug derived from a two-word name should produce a single hyphen between the words.

input.user_message: Look up Albert Einstein on Wikipedia

judge_context: Slug should be 'albert-einstein' — a simple two-word title with no special characters. The extract must be used verbatim from the tool response, not paraphrased.

IMPORTANT — file visibility: This skill writes a standalone markdown file to the working folder. That file will NOT appear in the file changes summary (which only tracks research.json and tree.gedcomx.json). If the skill called wikipedia_search successfully and its text response confirms saving a file with a correctly slugified name, treat file creation as fully successful. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for '(no file changes)' — that is expected behavior for this skill.

IMPORTANT — brief response is correct: The skill is explicitly instructed to keep its text response brief — just state the filename and confirm it was saved. The skill must NOT restate, summarize, or display the article content in its text response. A short confirmation like 'Saved the Wikipedia summary to albert-einstein.md' is the CORRECT and COMPLETE response. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for a brief response that omits article content.
